title: Car Fleet
problem_statement: Given target and pairs of position and speed for cars on a road, count how many car fleets will arrive at the target.

examples.explanation: Cars at 10 and 8 form fleets separately, remaining cars join into one more fleet.

intuition: Compute arrival times and use a monotonic stack from the furthest car back to group cars into fleets.

approaches.name: Monotonic stack of arrival times
approaches.description: Sort cars by starting position descending, then count a new fleet when a car’s arrival time is greater than the fleet ahead.
approaches.why_not: Simulating all cars individually can be slow and unnecessary.

interview_qa.q: Why sort cars by position descending?
interview_qa.a: Processing from the car closest to the target allows you to determine whether following cars catch up.
A new fleet forms when a following car arrives later than the current fleet ahead.
